Title: Create Project Successfully with Required Fields

Test Data: Project Name: TestProject001, Organization: GlobalOrg, Classification: CONFIDENTIAL

Steps: 1. Enter username: testuser; 2. Enter password: WrongPass123! (incorrect); 3. Click Login
Expected Results: 1. Password hash comparison fails; 2. Error message (generic): 'Invalid username or password'; 3. No password leak; 4. Login form remains; 5. Failed attempt counted for rate limiting

Title: Rate Limit - Disable After 5 Failed Attempts
Objective: Verify brute force protection (15-min lockout)
Preconditions: 1. testuser account exists; 2. Attacker makes 5 consecutive failed login attempts
Steps: 1. Attempt login with wrong password; 2. Repeat 4 more times (5 total); 3. Sixth attempt at 1-min mark; 4. Wait 15 minutes; 5. Sixth attempt at 16-min mark
Expected Results: Attempts 1-5: Failed login messages; Attempt 6 at 1-min: Error 'Account temporarily locked. Try again in 15 minutes'; Attempt 6 at 16-min: Login allowed again

Title: Logout Successfully Clears Session
Objective: Verify session termination on logout
Preconditions: 1. User authenticated and logged in; 2. on Projects dashboard; 3. localStorage has token
Steps: 1. Click user menu (top-right); 2. Click 'Logout'
Expected Results: 1. System clears authentication token from localStorage; 2. Session terminated on backend; 3. User redirected to login page; 4. Attempting to access /projects redirected to /login; 5. Protected routes no longer accessible; 6. Success message optional: 'You have been logged out'

Title: Prevent Unauthenticated Access to Protected Routes
Objective: Verify auth guard blocks unauthorized navigation
Preconditions: 1. User not authenticated (no token in localStorage); 2. attempting direct URL access to /projects
Test Data: User: anonymous, Token: null, URL: http://localhost:4200/projects
Steps: 1. Open new browser/incognito; 2. Navigate directly to /projects; 3. Observe redirection
Expected Results: 1. Auth guard intercepts navigation; 2. isAuthenticated signal = false; 3. User redirected to /login; 4. URL changes to http://localhost:4200/login; 5. Error message optional: 'You must log in to continue'

Feature: Attach Authentication Headers to API Requests
Title: Verify Auth Header Injected on All API Calls
Objective: Verify interceptor adds Authorization header
Preconditions: 1. User authenticated and has valid token; 2. About to make API call
Steps: 1. Authenticated user navigates to Projects; 2. ProjectService.getProjects() called; 3. Inspect HTTP request (browser dev tools)
Expected Results: 1. All HTTP GET/POST/PUT/DELETE requests include 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header; 2. Header not present on unauthenticated requests; 3. Backend receives header and validates token; 4. API returns 200 with data; 5. If token expired: backend returns 401, interceptor redirects to login

Title: Verify CORS Headers Allow Frontend Requests
Objective: Verify CORS configuration allows cross-origin requests
Preconditions: 1. Spring Boot backend running on localhost:8080; 2. Angular frontend running on localhost:4200; 3. CORS configuration active
Test Data: Frontend Origin: http://localhost:4200, Allowed Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
Steps: 1. Frontend makes HTTP GET request to /api/projects; 2. Browser includes 'Origin: http://localhost:4200' header; 3. Backend processes request
Expected Results: 1. Backend response includes 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:4200'; 2. Response includes 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE'; 3. Response includes 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true'; 4. Frontend receives response without CORS error; 5. Requests succeed; 6. Other origins (e.g., malicious.com) blocked with CORS error

Title: Complete Full Assessment Workflow 8-Step Wizard
Objective: Verify end-to-end wizard progression and step validation
Preconditions: 1. Project created; 2. Assessment initialized with STARTED status; 3. Wizard page displayed at Step 1
Steps: 1. Step 1 - Enter project name, version, organization; Click Next; 2. Step 2 - Enter description, objectives; Click Next; 3. Step 3 - Add 2 business assets; Click Next; 4. Step 4 - Add 1 supporting asset linked to business asset; Click Next; 5. Step 5 - Create 2 threats (manual or AI); Click Next; 6. Step 6 - Create 2 vulnerabilities; Click Next; 7. Step 7 - Create 2 risks; Click Next; 8. Step 8 - Add 1 mitigation; Click Finalize
Expected Results: 1. After each step: Validation passes; Next button enabled; User advances; 2. Current step highlighted in sidebar; 3. Completed steps marked with checkmarks; 4. Step-specific data persisted (refresh browser; step data retained); 5. Can navigate backward without re-validation; 6. Finalize button enabled after Step 8; 7. Click Finalize: Assessment status → FINALIZED; 8. Report generation option available; 9. Assessment complete with all workflow data

Title: Prevent Advance to Next Step if Validation Fails
Objective: Verify step validation blocks progression on missing mandatory data
Preconditions: 1. User on Step 1 (Basic Info); 2. Project Name field empty
Test Data: Step 1 Data: Name empty, Org filled, Classification selected
Steps: 1. Leave project name empty; 2. Fill organization and classification; 3. Click Next button
Expected Results: 1. Next button attempts click but form validates first; 2. Validation error: 'Project Name is required'; 3. Next NOT executed; 4. User remains on Step 1; 5. Error message displayed prominently; 6. Form field highlighted with red border

Title: Wizard Step Progress Persists Across Sessions
Objective: Verify state preservation on app restart
Preconditions: 1. User completes Steps 1-3 of wizard; 2. Closes browser; 3. Reopens app; 4. Logs in; 5. Navigates to same project
Steps: 1. Record steps completed before close (Step 1: Basic info, Step 2: Context, Step 3: Assets); 2. Close browser window; 3. Reopen app; 4. Log in with same credentials; 5. Open same project 'TestProject001'; 6. Wizard displayed
Expected Results: 1. Wizard shows current step = 4; 2. Steps 1-3 marked as completed (checkmarks visible); 3. Previously entered data in Steps 1-3 still present (query database); 4. Can navigate back to Step 1 and verify data; 5. No data loss on session restore; 6. User can continue from Step 4
